name: PSEUDOMYXOMA PERITONEI
definition: "jelly belly" = "gelatinous ascites" slow insidious accumulation of large amounts of thick mucinous or gelatinous material on benign / borderline / low-grade malignant epithelial cells from ruptured mucocele of the mucinous tumor, cystadenoma, tumor with low malignant potential, invasive carcinoma
distribution: posterior fixation of bowel loops + mesentery
discriminating_features: posterior fixation of bowel loops + mesentery
several thin-walled cystic masses of different size throughout abdominal cavity:

general: posterior fixation of bowel loops + mesentery
several thin-walled cystic masses of different size throughout abdominal cavity:
Hellmer sign = focal collection of mucin in right subhepatic space displacing tip of liver
central displacement of ascending + descending colon and lateral displacement of
annular / semicircular calcifications (rare but highly suggestive)
CT: intraperitoneal collection of very low attenuation (common) / soft-tissue density (rare)
US: nonmobile echoes from proteinaceous / bloody / fibrinous exudate
